children.name: Aepypodius
children.description: Aepypodius — a genus of megapodes in the family Megapodiidae.

children.name: Aepypodius arfakianus
children.description: The wattled brushturkey is a species of bird in the family Megapodiidae found year-round in New Guinea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

children.name: Aepypodius bruijnii
children.description: The Waigeo brushturkey or Bruijn's brushturkey, is a large brownish-black megapode with a bare red facial skin, red comb, maroon rump, and chestnut brown below. There are two elongated red wattles on the back of the head and a long wattle on the foredeck. Both sexes are similar.

children.name: Alectura
children.description: Large, dark-plumaged megapodes with a prominent bare red head and a laterally compressed tail. They are widespread in eastern Australia and are famous for building enormous incubation mounds.

children.name: Eulipoa
children.description: A monotypic genus of megapode endemic to the Moluccas.

children.name: Leipoa
children.description: A monotypic genus of shy, intricately patterned megapodes that inhabit the arid mallee scrublands of southern Australia. They are renowned for their precisely regulated incubation mounds.

children.name: Macrocephalon
children.description: A monotypic genus endemic to Sulawesi, instantly recognisable by the prominent helmet-like casque on its head. It is one of the most distinctive and endangered megapodes.

children.name: Megapodius
children.description: A widespread genus of medium-sized, drab-plumaged megapodes found across Southeast Asia, Australasia and the Pacific islands. They are named for their habit of scratching through deep forest litter.

children.name: Megapodius decollatus
children.description: The New Guinea scrubfowl or New Guinea megapode is a species of bird in the family Megapodiidae. It is found in New Guinea, mostly in the northern half. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

children.name: Megapodius eremita
children.description: The Melanesian scrubfowl or Melanesian megapode is a megapode species that is endemic to islands within Melanesia. The Melanesian scrubfowl has a unique strategy of egg incubation in which it relies on environmental heat sources. This bird species is culturally important for Indigenous peoples in Melanesia.

children.name: Megapodius geelvinkianus
children.description: #REDIRECT Biak scrubfowl Category:Taxa named by Adolf Bernhard Meyer.

children.name: Megapodius laferrieri
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sulu Archipelago.

children.name: Megapodius laperouse
children.description: The Micronesian megapode or Micronesian scrubfowl is a megapode which inhabits islands of the Western Pacific Ocean.

children.name: Megapodius layardi
children.description: The Vanuatu megapode or Vanuatu scrubfowl is a species of bird in the family Megapodiidae. It was formerly known as the New Hebrides scrubfowl. It is found only in Vanuatu.

children.name: Megapodius leakeyorum
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sula Islands.

children.name: Megapodius tenebrosus
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to the Tanimbar Islands, Indonesia.

children.name: Megapodius tenimberensis
children.description: The Tanimbar megapode or Tanimbar scrubfowl is a small megapode endemic to the Tanimbar Islands of Indonesia. It is sometimes considered to be a subspecies of the orange-footed scrubfowl, Megapodius reinwardt.
